rules.title: Unchecked Return Value in Security Critical Calls
rules.rationale: Prevents silent failures in cryptographic or memory management routines that could lead to inconsistent state or memory corruption.
preconditions: Function signature is mapped in the domain-specific security-critical API dictionary.
Current target includes APIs such as fd_sha256, fd_workspace_alloc, fd_quic.
rules.detection_logic: Match calls to ballet, verify, fd_workspace, or fd_quic APIs where the return value is not used in a subsequent comparison, conditional branch, or assertion.
false_positive_patterns: Return values from functions that are void or guaranteed to succeed in current configuration.
Functions called within assertions disabled in production builds.
rules.remediation_hint: Always check return codes and use macros such as FD_TEST or propagate error codes to the caller.
rules.minimal_example_good: if( FD_UNLIKELY( !fd_sha256_hash( data, sz, hash ) ) ) return ERR_CRYPTO;

rules.title: Use-After-Free in Shared Memory Pools
rules.rationale: Prevents memory corruption in tile-based architecture where pool elements are released back to shared workspaces and then accessed.
preconditions: Code path includes fd_executor pool elements or Tango mcache, dcache shared regions.
Release semantics for tracked API indicate object is no longer valid for dereference.
rules.detection_logic: Track object lifecycle in shared workspaces and flag pointer access after release calls such as fd_executor_release.
false_positive_patterns: Pointer is reassigned before dereference.
Release call decrements refcount only and object remains valid.
rules.remediation_hint: Nullify pointers after release and ensure refcounting and ownership transfer are explicit and atomic where required.

rules.title: Undefined Behavior in Shift Operations from External Input
rules.rationale: Prevents remote connection hangs or RTT estimation corruption in QUIC and networking stack due to exponents greater than or equal to word size.
preconditions: Shift exponent originates from network ingress code paths.
No sanitizer pass guarantees bounded exponent before this point.
rules.detection_logic: Identify shift operations where exponent dataflow reaches expression from fd_quic or fd_net ingress without explicit bound check such as exponent < 64.
false_positive_patterns: Hardcoded exponent literals.
Exponent value is masked, for example with 0x3f, before shift.
rules.remediation_hint: Validate shift exponent is strictly less than word size before the shift.

rules.title: C float-to-unsigned cast (Rust parity heuristic)
rules.rationale: Prevents consensus splits between Agave and Firedancer caused by different handling of out-of-range or non-finite float to unsigned integer conversion when the cast operand is actually floating-point.
preconditions: Code is in consensus-critical paths such as runtime, rent, stake, rewards, or bank-hash related logic.
Heuristic detects a direct C cast to unsigned integer where the operand text suggests float/double (C adapter has no type graph).
rules.detection_logic: Find casts to unsigned integer types without fd_rust_cast (or related wrappers) where the operand snippet contains float/double keywords, float-like literals, *_double / *_float identifiers, or other heuristic FP markers.
false_positive_patterns: Integer-only casts such as (ulong)bitmask or (ulong)field from bit-struct or int storage.
Operands that are int or pointer but use names resembling float tokens.
Value is proven in-range and non-negative by prior checks.
Floating-point value is telemetry-only and not consensus-relevant.
rules.remediation_hint: If the operand is floating-point, use fd_rust_cast_double_to_ulong or an equivalent saturating helper for Rust-aligned semantics.

rules.title: Missing Reserved Account in Unwritable List
rules.rationale: Prevents leaders from allowing writes to reserved accounts and producing block hash mismatches with Agave.
preconditions: Rule runs on fd_pack or accounts database unwritable registry implementation.
Reference list from Agave is available for the same target network and feature-gate set.

external_source_ref.notes: Use exact commit hash or release tag for reproducible comparison.

rules.detection_logic: Cross-reference local reserved account keys against the pinned Agave reserved account key registry and flag missing entries.
false_positive_patterns: Account is reserved upstream but feature gate is not active for current cluster profile.
rules.remediation_hint: Synchronize local unwritable account list with pinned Agave reserved account registry and revalidate feature-gate conditions.

rules.title: Function Type Mismatch in Sysvar Decode Callbacks
rules.rationale: Prevents undefined behavior that could lead to crashes or sandbox boundary violations when decoding sysvars through function pointers.
preconditions: Assignment targets fd_sysvar_pos_t decode callback in runtime sysvar code.
Expected typedef signature is available from fd_sysvar_cache headers.
rules.detection_logic: Perform static type signature check for function pointer assignments to sysvar decode callbacks and flag return or parameter mismatches.
false_positive_patterns: Generic context pointer patterns where concrete callback signature still matches typedef.
rules.remediation_hint: Ensure callback signature exactly matches typedef in fd_sysvar_cache headers.

rules.title: Bitwise AND for Flag Normalization in Consensus Hashes
rules.rationale: Using bitwise AND instead of boolean double-negation on flag fields causes Firedancer to produce different bank hash values from Agave when the flag byte is non-zero but not equal to 1.
preconditions: Variable is a known boolean-semantic flag field (executable, writable, is_native, is_signer, is_invoked).
Statement is part of account state hashing or serialization logic.
rules.detection_logic: Match usage of bitwise AND with 1 or 0x1 on known boolean flag field names where the Agave reference uses boolean normalization via double negation.
false_positive_patterns: Flags intended for raw bitmask storage where bitwise precision is required.
Non-consensus telemetry or logging logic.
rules.remediation_hint: Replace field & 1 with !!field or explicit (bool) cast to guarantee standard boolean normalization matching Agave behavior.

rules.title: Missing Limit on Invisible QUIC Protocol Frames
rules.rationale: Failure to limit frames that are invisible to the application layer allows attackers to exhaust CPU resources without sending useful data.
preconditions: Logic handles raw QUIC frames in the frame parser loop.
Code path is in the QUIC packet ingress path.
rules.detection_logic: Identify frame processing calls inside loops that lack a per-packet or per-connection frame count cap in the surrounding statement window.
false_positive_patterns: High-level application frames already credit-gated by SWQoS.
Loops with documented frame budget already enforced at a higher level.
rules.remediation_hint: Implement a counter to limit the number of protocol frames consumed per single UDP packet.
rules.minimal_example_good: while( frames_left && frame_count++ < MAX_FRAMES ) { parse_and_handle( frame ); }

rules.title: Unverified Hash Label in Snapshot Loader
rules.rationale: Ignoring discrepancies between archive hash labels and loaded bank hashes allows the ingestion of corrupted or malicious state snapshots.
preconditions: Execution occurs within the snapshot loader component.
Snapshot archive access and bank hash are both present in scope.
rules.detection_logic: Flag paths where snapshot metadata labels are read but not compared against the computed hash of the loaded bank before completing boot.
false_positive_patterns: Development or test builds where hash verification is explicitly bypassed via compile flags.
rules.remediation_hint: Mandate a comparison between the hash label in the archive and the bank loaded snapshot hash before finishing boot.

rules.title: Cross-Tile Flow Control Logic Corruption
rules.rationale: Writing to sequence numbers of links not owned by the current tile breaks the sandboxing model and allows side-channel denial of service.
preconditions: Tile maps the metric_in workspace as read-write.
Current tile is not a management tile (metric, diag).
rules.detection_logic: Track fseq pointer writes and flag cases where the fseq object corresponds to a tile link the current tile is not a consumer of.
false_positive_patterns: Management tiles such as metric or diag that are intended to monitor or reset global state.
rules.remediation_hint: Isolate return-path sequence numbers into tile-specific workspaces rather than a shared global metrics workspace.

rules.title: Missing Handshake Eviction Strategy
rules.rationale: Malicious clients can occupy all handshake slots by sending PINGs, blocking new legitimate connections.
preconditions: QUIC server code manages hs_pool.
Code path handles new connection establishment.
rules.detection_logic: Flag QUIC connection initiation code that rejects new handshakes when the pool is full without first attempting eviction of stale or incomplete sessions.
false_positive_patterns: Environments where max_handshakes is configured high enough to exceed NIC capacity.
Pools that implement eviction at a higher abstraction layer.
rules.remediation_hint: Implement a LIFO or oldest-incomplete eviction policy for the handshake pool when all slots are occupied.

rules.title: Unsafe Bank Reference Counting in Shared Memory
rules.rationale: Bank structures are accessed concurrently across tiles in the replay/banks component. Non-atomic refcount operations cause data races leading to use-after-free or double-free on bank state.
preconditions: Code executes within the replay or banks subsystem.
Statement manipulates a field named ref_cnt, ref_count, or bank_ref on a shared bank structure.
rules.detection_logic: Identify non-atomic increments or decrements of bank reference counter fields (ref_cnt, ref_count, bank_ref) that are not wrapped in an atomic primitive or an fd_bank_ref helper.
false_positive_patterns: Refcount updates protected by a global workspace lock.
Read-only access to bank metadata where lifetime is guaranteed by a parent tile.
rules.remediation_hint: Use __atomic_fetch_add or fd_bank_ref_inc for all refcount operations in shared workspaces.

rules.title: Race Condition in Funk Database io_uring Submissions
rules.rationale: Funk uses io_uring for high-performance I/O. Multiple tiles submitting to the same ring without per-tile instances or explicit synchronization causes account state corruption.
preconditions: Interaction with the Funk or Vinyl database via asynchronous I/O.
Multiple execution tiles share access to io_uring state.
rules.detection_logic: Flag calls to io_uring_submit on a ring that is not clearly tile-private (not referenced through a tile-local struct or per-tile index).
false_positive_patterns: Single-threaded management tiles (e.g., store tile) that own the ring exclusively.
rules.remediation_hint: Ensure each execution tile uses a private io_uring instance or strictly partitioned SQE ranges.

rules.title: Incorrect ALT Resolution Slot Window
rules.rationale: Firedancer uses a 512-slot window for ALT deactivation. Using a different constant causes transaction rejection discrepancies with Agave.
preconditions: Logic is in the resolve tile that filters transactions based on ALT availability.
A numeric slot-distance constant is compared against the deactivation slot.
rules.detection_logic: Match slot-distance comparisons in ALT resolution context that use a numeric literal other than 512.
false_positive_patterns: Test mocks using smaller windows for speed.
Internal metrics tracking that do not affect transaction dropping.
rules.remediation_hint: Use exactly the 512 recent slot hashes window to match Agave ALT inclusion semantics.

rules.title: Non-Atomic Identity Switch Coordination
rules.rationale: Live identity switching (keyswitch) requires: pause PoH, flush shreds, update signers, resume PoH. Violating this order causes a leader to produce blocks with inconsistent identities.
preconditions: Execution path covers the set_identity command or keyswitch housekeeping.
Both poh halt and sign tile update calls are present in the function.
rules.detection_logic: Flag functions where the sign tile key update call appears before a confirmed poh halt call, indicating the forbidden SIGN_UPDATE -> POH_HALT ordering.
false_positive_patterns: Initial boot sequence where no blocks are yet in-flight.
rules.remediation_hint: Enforce the state machine: HALT -> FLUSH -> UPDATE -> RESUME.

rules.title: Missing Rogue Key Protection in Alpenglow Aggregation
rules.rationale: Alpenglow uses BLS12-381 for signature aggregation. Summing public keys without proofs-of-possession allows rogue key subtraction attacks that compromise consensus.
preconditions: Consensus voting path uses the Votor component of Alpenglow.
BLS12-381 aggregate public key is computed from a list of validator keys.
rules.detection_logic: Flag BLS key aggregation calls where no verify_pop or proof-of-possession check appears in the surrounding statement window.
false_positive_patterns: Aggregations where public keys are pre-vetted during validator admission (VAT).
Inner aggregation utilities called only from a wrapper that already validates proofs.
rules.remediation_hint: Implement proof-of-possession checks for all validator public keys before admitting them to the Alpenglow voting set.
rules.minimal_example_good: if( !bls_verify_pop( pk ) ) reject_validator( pk ); aggregate_pk = bls_sum( validator_pks );

rules.title: Semantic Mismatch on UnsupportedProgramId Error
rules.rationale: Firedancer and Agave must return identical error codes for unknown programs. A mismatch in UnsupportedProgramId error priority produces different bank hashes.
preconditions: Instruction execution logic is in the Flamenco VM or runtime dispatcher.
An error code is returned for an unrecognized or unavailable program ID.
rules.detection_logic: Flag error return statements in program dispatch logic that use a generic code such as ERR_NOT_FOUND, ERR_INVALID, or ERR_UNKNOWN when the Agave-compatible code should be ERR_UNSUPPORTED_PROGRAM_ID.
false_positive_patterns: Programs that are gated and not yet active on the target cluster.
Test harnesses that canonicalize error codes before comparison.
rules.remediation_hint: Use differential testing (solfuzz-agave) to validate error code priority matches Agave exactly for all invalid program ID paths.

rules.title: Unchecked Signed Integer Overflow in Consensus Logic
rules.rationale: In C, signed integer overflow is undefined behavior. If Firedancer overflow paths differ from Agave behavior, bank hash divergence can occur in reward or rent calculations.
preconditions: Statement performs arithmetic on signed integer types (int, long, ssize_t).
Code path is in runtime or consensus-sensitive math flow.
rules.detection_logic: Flag signed arithmetic expressions that use +, -, or * without overflow-safe helpers or explicit overflow checks.
false_positive_patterns: Operand bounds are provably constrained before arithmetic by strict range checks.
rules.remediation_hint: Use __builtin_add_overflow or project wrappers to enforce explicit overflow handling aligned with Agave behavior.

rules.title: Unspecified Order of Evaluation with Side Effects
rules.rationale: C argument evaluation order is unspecified, while Rust uses stable left-to-right evaluation. Multiple side-effect calls in one argument list can produce state divergence.
preconditions: Function call contains two or more nested function-call arguments.
Nested arguments update hash/state/shared data.
rules.detection_logic: Identify function calls where multiple argument expressions are side-effecting calls and are evaluated in a single call expression.
false_positive_patterns: Nested argument calls are pure and do not mutate state.
rules.remediation_hint: Materialize side-effect call results into locals in explicit order before invoking the parent call.
rules.minimal_example_good: uint h1 = update_hash(a); uint h2 = update_hash(b); process_tx( h1, h2 );

rules.title: Implicit Padding in Protocol-Mapped Structures
rules.rationale: Implicit compiler padding in protocol-mapped C structs can diverge from expected Rust layout and corrupt shred/account decoding.
preconditions: Struct is cast from network or storage buffer.
Struct has no explicit packed/alignment control and no size assertion nearby.
rules.detection_logic: Flag protocol-oriented struct declarations that are used in raw buffer casts without packed/alignment annotation or equivalent size guard.
false_positive_patterns: Internal structs not used for wire or disk binary mapping.
rules.remediation_hint: Use explicit layout control (__attribute__((packed)) or static_assert layout checks) and align with Agave type sizes.
rules.minimal_example_bad: struct shred { uchar type; ulong slot; };
rules.minimal_example_good: struct __attribute__((packed)) shred { uchar type; ulong slot; };

rules.title: Signed Division/Modulo Rounding Mismatch
rules.rationale: Signed division and modulo semantics can diverge from protocol-intended Euclidean math. Using raw operators in consensus math may change distribution outputs.
preconditions: Expression uses / or % on signed values.
Code path is reward/rent or other protocol-sensitive math.
rules.detection_logic: Flag signed / or % operations that do not use explicit Euclidean/floor helper semantics.
false_positive_patterns: Operands are guaranteed non-negative and protocol rounding mode is irrelevant.
rules.remediation_hint: Use explicit division helpers that encode the protocol rounding mode.

rules.title: Unaligned Memory Access in Flamenco VM
rules.rationale: VM load/store without strict alignment checks can diverge from expected eBPF behavior and introduce safety or consensus risk.
preconditions: Statement performs pointer-cast memory load/store from vm memory region.
No nearby explicit alignment guard is present.
rules.detection_logic: Match vm memory pointer-cast loads/stores that rely on computed address without explicit alignment check in surrounding statements.
false_positive_patterns: Feature-gated paths where unaligned access is explicitly allowed and validated.
rules.remediation_hint: Enforce alignment checks on LD/ST accesses in VM execution paths before dereferencing cast pointers.
rules.minimal_example_good: if( FD_UNLIKELY( addr & 0x7 ) ) return FD_VM_ERR_UNALIGNED; *(ulong *)(vm->mem + addr) = val;
